title: Velvety Canadian Tofu Curry
description: A buttery, dense, energizing Canadian dish that blends Massaman curry paste with maple-infused coconut milk and blanched tofu for a vegan, kosher-friendly dinner.
instructions: Press the tofu for 15 minutes, then cut into 1‑inch cubes.
Heat the coconut oil in a large skillet over medium heat; add the Massaman curry paste and cook for 1–2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the tofu cubes and stir‑fry until lightly browned on all sides, about 3–4 minutes.
Pour in the coconut milk, maple syrup, and soy sauce; add the ground ginger and bring to a gentle simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the curry simmer for 10 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken.
Stir in the peanut butter until fully incorporated and the sauce becomes silky.
Add the cooked brown rice, tossing to coat each grain and heat through, about 2 minutes.
Season with salt to taste, adjusting sweetness or salt as desired.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.
